title: Go to Joseph!
summary: “And when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread: and Pharaoh said unto all the Egyptians, Go unto Joseph; what he saith to you, do.” (Gen 41:55). We are the Josephs of the Lord Jesus Christ in the world today! Few righteous people have been so abused and mistreated as Joseph. Consider it through the lens of two of Joseph’s garments: the blood-stained coat of many colors, and the garment that Potiphar’s wife showed to her husband. The first was to lie about his death and the second to lie about his sexual conduct. “Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all” (Psalm 34:19). Because Joseph had the favor of God and would deliver Egypt and the world from starvation, the Devil declared war on him. Let us learn from humble Joseph. He never carried a grudge or sought revenge throughout these terrible ordeals. Joseph points to us who are custodians and guardians of the knowledge of the salvation of God through Jesus Christ. Let us pray for the patience and forgiveness that characterized Joseph. One of these days, Pharaoh himself will command Egypt to “Got to Joseph!”
